channel.title: NPR Topics: Technology
channel.description: Latest technology news and breakthroughs in technology, science, and industry. Download the NPR Technology podcast and Technology RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: SpaceX IPO makes history as largest ever. Stock gains 19% on first day
item.description: The initial public offering from the rocket and AI company raised some $75 billion, making the company one of the biggest in the world — and likely making Elon Musk a trillionaire.
item.content:encoded:
The initial public offering from the rocket and AI company raised some $75 billion, making the company one of the biggest in the world — and likely making Elon Musk a trillionaire.

(Image credit: RONALDO SCHEMIDT)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: SpaceX blasts off with a record-breaking $75 billion IPO
item.description: Elon Musk's rocket company, recently merged with xAI, raised $75 billion in its initial public stock offering. It's the first of a trio of mega-IPOs from AI companies expected this year.
item.content:encoded:
Elon Musk's rocket company, recently merged with xAI, raised $75 billion in its initial public stock offering. It's the first of a trio of mega-IPOs from AI companies expected this year.

(Image credit: Eric Gay)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: The theory taking the rich by storm: China funds data center haters
item.description: Tech millionaires claim China is behind a wave of local opposition to U.S. data centers, while providing little direct evidence.
item.content:encoded:
Tech millionaires claim China is behind a wave of local opposition to U.S. data centers, while providing little direct evidence.

(Image credit: Natalie Behring)
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: ICE denies having a protester database. But a letter to Congress sheds more light
item.description: In a previously unpublicized letter to Congress, the newly departed head of ICE said the agency collects data on people suspected of potentially unlawful activity, which could include protesters.
item.content:encoded:
In a previously unpublicized letter to Congress, the newly departed head of ICE said the agency collects data on people suspected of potentially unlawful activity, which could include protesters.

(Image credit: Joseph Prezioso)
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block

item.title: AI development is driving economic inequality, says tech critic Karen Hao
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with tech journalist Karen Hao about the Pope's recent warnings that AI companies represent a new form of colonialism.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with tech journalist Karen Hao about the Pope's recent warnings that AI companies represent a new form of colonialism.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Hey, Siri: Apple just announced a long-awaited AI update
item.description: At its annual developers' conference, Apple put the spotlight on new AI features, while highlighting security and child safety — and critiquing the company's AI competitors.
item.content:encoded:
At its annual developers' conference, Apple put the spotlight on new AI features, while highlighting security and child safety — and critiquing the company's AI competitors.

(Image credit: Noah Berger)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: Kalshi and Polymarket crack down on paid influencers claiming election fraud
item.description: Influencers are using prediction market odds to sow doubt in vote counting, in some cases in posts paid for by the companies themselves.
item.content:encoded:
Influencers are using prediction market odds to sow doubt in vote counting, in some cases in posts paid for by the companies themselves.

(Image credit: Jill Connelly)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Most K-12 teachers say AI's impact on education will eclipse the internet or computers
item.description: A new NPR/Ipsos poll shows many teachers are using AI to save time, but a majority are also worried the technology is making it harder for students to learn to think for themselves.
item.content:encoded: A new NPR/Ipsos poll shows many teachers are using AI to save time, but a majority are also worried the technology is making it harder for students to learn to think for themselves.
item.dc:creator: Lee V. Gaines

item.title: I wrote about George Santos. Then he made a violent threat and lied about it
item.description: NPR reported on new federal investigations examining the former Congressman's bets on the prediction market site Kalshi. Then he threatened the NPR reporter who broke the story.
item.content:encoded:
NPR reported on new federal investigations examining the former Congressman's bets on the prediction market site Kalshi. Then he threatened the NPR reporter who broke the story.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson/AP)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: What do you actually get when you pay for AI?
item.description: Just 3% of U.S. households pay for AI for personal use. Sign ups are growing — even though Americans have subscription fatigue.
item.content:encoded:
Just 3% of U.S. households pay for AI for personal use. Sign ups are growing — even though Americans have subscription fatigue.

(Image credit: Charly Triballeau/AFP)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha
